name: Gibraltar (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the upper band — from the historic civic arms
the lower band and the castle — from the civic arms granted by Queen Isabella I of Castile in 1502
the golden key beneath the castle — Gibraltar's epithet as 'the key' to the Mediterranean

description: Two unequal horizontal stripes — white above (two-thirds the height) and red below (one-third) — with a red three-towered castle suspended a golden key in the centre, drawn from Gibraltar's historic civic arms.
history: The flag was officially granted to Gibraltar by Royal Warrant of Queen Elizabeth II on 8 November 1982. It is a banner of the historic civic coat of arms originally granted to the city by Queen Isabella I of Castile on 10 July 1502, which was retained after the British capture of Gibraltar in 1704 and is one of very few non-British flags flown by a British Overseas Territory as its territorial flag.

features.symbolism: the colours of the historic coat of arms granted to Gibraltar by the Catholic Monarchs in 1502 — a red castle on white

A red castle with three towers, the central tower taller, taken directly from the historic arms granted to Gibraltar by Queen Isabella I of Castile in 1502 — representing Gibraltar's strategic fortress role.

features.role: golden key hanging from the castle
features.symbolism: A golden key suspended from the central tower of the castle — symbolising Gibraltar's status as the 'key to the Mediterranean', controlling the strait between the Atlantic Ocean and the Mediterranean Sea.
